answers: Building a website for his sister's custom hat company — she buys blank hats and sews custom leather patches onto them.
She is paying him and he enjoys building websites.
The business outcome is secondary — Andrew is not actively involved beyond the build.
Wants the work to look professional, clean, and amazing. Refuses crude or shoddy output.
Perfectionist by nature — work that doesn't meet his standard reflects poorly on him as a builder. Quality is identity.
deep_why.summary: Client work for family. The deeper driver is craft pride — Andrew holds a perfectionist standard and the site must reflect that, regardless of whether the business takes off.

north_star.metric: Fully functional, zero-defect deploy — all site features work without issues

values.name: Polish
values.meaning: No crude output. Every detail reflects a perfectionist standard.

values.name: Overall look
values.meaning: Visual quality is the primary deliverable. Must stand out from generic shop/POS sites.

values.name: Good animations
values.meaning: Animations elevate the feel and tie the aesthetic together — not decoration, structure.

values.name: Reliability
values.meaning: Zero-defect deploy. Everything functions. No broken flows.

values.name: Speed
values.meaning: Site performance — fast load, snappy interactions.

floors.1: A website for a custom hat company that takes blank hats and applies custom leather patches.
floors.2: A premium e-commerce site for a custom hat brand — built to look and feel unlike any generic shop site, with polished UI and intentional animations.
floors.3: Client work for family. The goal is a fully functional, visually distinctive storefront that reflects professional craft — not just another Shopify clone.

scenes: The site is live. It loads fast. The UI is immediately different from every other hat shop online — the animations are purposeful, the layout is clean, and nothing is broken. Andrew looks at it and it meets his standard. Sister can share the URL and it holds up.
